case_title: CHARLENE CUNNINGHAM WHITMORE VS. WILLIAM EDWARD WHITMORE JR et al

MINI-MINUTES FOR AUG-22-2002 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 218

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF WHITMORE, CHARLENE CUNNINGHAM RECOVER FROM DEFENDANT WHITMORE JR, WILLIAM EDWARD SEE SCANNED DOC FOR DETAILS OF THE INTERLOCUTORY JUDGMENT AND SALE OF THE REAL PROPERTY AT 300 THIRD STREET UNIT 312 AND DETAILS OF THE DISTRIBUTION OF PROCEEDS THEREFROM. NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT AND AFFIDAVIT OF MAILING FILED
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF OCT-18-2002 CONTINUED TO FEB-28-2003 AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 212. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: THE COURT ORDERS THE PARTITION OF THE PROPERTY BY PRIVATE SALE LOCATED AT 300 THIRD STREET UNIT #312 SAN FRANCISCO, CA. JILL HACKER IS APPOINTED TO SELL THE PROPERTY IN THE MANNER REQUIRED IN PRIVATE SALES OF REAL PROPERTY ESTATES OF DECEASED PERSONS AS REQUIRED BY LAW. DEFENDANT WILLIAM EDWARD WHITMORE, JR. SHALL PERMIT ACCESS TO THE PROPERTY NOTED ON TWENTY FOUR HOURS NOTICE FOR PURPOSES OF MARKETING AND SHOWING IT TO POTENTIAL BUYERS ETC. THE COURT RETAINS JURISDICTION OVER THIS MATTER AND PLAINTIFF MAY APPLY EX-PARTE FOR FURTHER ORDERS CONSISTENT WITH AND PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTINS 872.120-872.130. AFTER MAKING SALE OF THE PROPERTY JILL HACKER IS TO REPORT OT THIS COURT HER PROCEEDINGS THEREON AND ON CONFIRMATION OF SALE, THE DEED TO THE REAL PROPERTY SHALL BE DELIVERED TO THE PURCHASER. DEFENDANT WILLIAM EDWARD WHITMORE SHALL VACATE THE PROPERTY NO LATER THAT THIRTY DAYS FROM THE DATE THIS COURT CONFIRMS THE SALE OF THIS PROPERTY. PROCEEDS OF THE SALE OF THE PROPERTY SHALL BE APPLIED IN THE FOLLOWING ORDER (A). TO PAY EXPENSES CONNECTED WITH THE SALE OF THE PROPERTY. (B.) TO PAY DEFENDANT HOME SAVINGS OF AMERICA, F.A. SUMS DUE AND TO BECOME DUE UNDER ITS DEED OF TRUST AS VARIFIED BY AFFIDAVIT AT THE TIME OF PAYMENT. (C.) TO PAY TO DEFENDANT MUSEUM PARC RESIDENTIAL HOMEOWNERS' ASSOCIATION THE SUMS DUE AND TO BECOME DUE UNDER ITS LIEN FOR ASSOCIATION DUES, AS VARIFIED BY AFFIDAVIT AT THE TIME OF PAYMENT. (D.) PAY LIENS OF THE CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO FOR BACK TAXES THAT ARE DUE BY AFFIDAVIT AT THE TIME OF PAYMENT. (E.) TO PAY PLAINTIFF CHARLENE CUNNINGHAM WHITMORE $16,326.00 FOR REIMBURSEMENT OF PAYMENTS MADE BY PLAINTIFF TO DEFENDANT HOME SAVINGS OF AMERICA F.A. AND TO DEFENDANT MUSEUM PARC HOMEOWNER'S ASOCIATION WITH INTEREST THEREON AT THE LEGAL RATE AS VARIFIED BY AFFIDAVIT AT TIME OF PAYMENT. AND TO PAY PLAINTIFF REASONABLE ATTORNEY FEES AND COST FOR LEGAL ACTION. SEE JUDGMENT AT LENGHTH FOR OTHER DETAILS.

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-20-2003 Off Calendar

Motion To Confirm Partition And Sale Of Real Property In Scd 218 On January 23, 2003.
Off Calendar Jan-24-2003 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
This cause came on regularly as scheduled for hearing this day. Commissioner: Loretta M. Norris; Clerk: P. Dias; Reporter: Ron Sinclair. Stephen K. Lightfoot, II, Esq. appeared for Plaintiff, Charlene Cunningham Whitmore. Defendant William E. Whitmore appeared in pro per. The Court hears evidence and grants the petition as prayed for. Order signed in open court. See scanned document.

Court Confirmation Of Sale Of Real Property In Scd 218 At 09:00 Am On December 26, 2002.
Off Calendar Dec-26-2002 Off Calendar

Exparte Application To Modify Order
Off Calendar Nov-18-2002 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause came on regularly as scheduled for hearing this day for Modification of Interlocutory Judgment - Sale of Real Property. Present: Commissioner Loretta M. Norris; Clerk: P. Dias; Reporter: Patrick Heald. Plaintiff was represented by Stephen K. Lightfoot, Esq. Also present, William Edward Whitmore, Jr. who was properly notified of this hearing by telephone and by a written letter. The Court having heard evidence, grants the Modification of Partition of Interlocutory Judgment regarding the terms of the sale. The Matter is set for December 17, 2002 for Court confirmation. Order signed in open court.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-07-2002 Continued To Feb-28-03 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

STATUS CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-15-2002 Continued To Oct-18-02 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 212

Default Prove-Up Partition Action
Off Calendar Aug-22-2002 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This cause comes on calendar this day for Default Prove-Up-Partition Action. Plaintiff represented by and through her counsel Stephen K. Lightfoot, II, Esq., presents evidence. Good cause appearing the court signs Interloctory Judgment - Sale of Property. Oral Motion for service by regular certified mail, granted. Counsel to prepare order for signature.
